summarization.a: The chart shows the relationship between the Si content and the film thickness, and the partial pressure of SiH4.

data_extraction.a:
| Partial pressure of SiH₄ [Pa] | Si contents [at.%] | Film thickness per cycle [nm/cycle] |
|---|---|---|
| 0.1 | 17 | 0.25 |
| 1 | 15 | 0.23 |
| 10 | 18 | 0.21 |
| 100 | 19 | 0.19 |

a.question: Is there a relation between the Si content and the film thickness per cycle?
How does Si content vary with the partial pressure of SiH4?
a.answer: As the partial pressure increases, so does the Si content, albeit slightly.

a.question: What are the Si content and film thickness when the partial pressure of SiH4 is 1 Pa?
a.answer: At that pressure, the Si content is closer to 15 at% and the film thickness is around 0.3 nm/cycle.

a.question: Is the dependence of the Si content using MOALD comparable with the MOVCD?
a.answer: In this case, the Si deposited through MOALD is not comparable with that deposited through MOCVD, because the latter increases logarithmically with the partial pressure of SiH4, which differs with the data shown in the figure.
